matches.x: ; ” CHAPTER yur, The companions of Ezra—He sendeth to ‘Iddo—He keepeth'a fast. HESE are now the chief of their fathers, and this is the genealogy of them that went up with me’ from Babylon, in the reign of Artaxerxes the king.

; ” CHAPTER yur, The companions of Ezra—He sendeth to ‘Iddo—He keepeth'a fast. HESE are now the chief of their fathers, and this is the genealogy of them that went up with me’ from Babylon, in the reign of Artaxerxes the king.

Now these are the heads of their fathers’ 8 houses, and this is the genealogy of them that went up with me from Babylon, in the reign of Artaxerxes the king. Of the sons of Phinehas, Gershom: of the sons of Ithamar, Daniel: of the sons of David, Hattush. Of the sons of 3 Shecaniah ; of the sons of Parosh, Zechariah : and with him were reckoned by genealogy of the males an hundred and fifty. Of…

It is possible that the Nethinim, mentioned with the priests and the Levites, were the deacons of ancient times. (See 1 Chronicles 9:2; Ezra 2:43; 7:7, 24; Nehemiah 10:28; 11:3.) We read of them in Ezra 8:20: "Also of the Nethinims, whom David and the princes (apostles?) had appointed [ordained] for the service of the Levites, two hundred and twenty Nethinims: all of them were expressed by name."…

there was no need for a temple presidency. This, then, is why Ezra and Nehemiah were required to choose such a presidency when they reconstructed the temple. Ezra appointed other officers as well, including a council of twelve: "So we fasted and besought our God for this: and he was intreated of us. Then I separated [ordained] twelve of the chief of the priests [i.e., high priests], Sherebiah,…

there was no need for a temple presidency. This, then, is why Ezra and Nehemiah were required to choose such a presidency when they reconstructed the temple. Ezra appointed other officers as well, including a council of twelve: "So we fasted and besought our God for this: and he was intreated of us. Now these are the heads of their fathers’ 8 houses, and this is the genealogy of them that went up with me from Babylon, in the reign of Artaxerxes the king. Of the sons of Phinehas, Gershom: of the sons of Ithamar, Daniel: of the sons of David, Hattush. Of the sons of 3 Shecaniah ; of the sons of Parosh, Zechariah : and with him were reckoned by genealogy of the males an hundred and fifty. Of…

we departed from the river of Ahava on the twelfth day of the first month--Computing from the time of their setting out to the period of their arrival, they occupied about four months on the way. (About the same amount of time as the first company of pioneers from Winter Quarters to Utah)

In Mic 6:11, what is a "deceitful weight"? A: This is a weight that a trader deceitfully represents as either a certain weight, or else equivalent to another weight, as Deuteronomy 25:13 shows.
